preamble: Glide formation (Iotation) is a process where a vowel loses its syllabic status and becomes a semi-vowel glide, typically occurring before another vowel or in unstressed positions.

examples.note: Stages in Romance development where unstressed 'a' before high vowels develops into a glide.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
